sprintName: Phase Modules: Planner, Contract, Generator, Evaluator
summary: Port the four agent-phase wrappers (planner.sh, contract.sh, generator.sh, evaluator.sh) to Python 3.8+ stdlib, preserving the EXACT output-tolerance logic for criteria counting, contract decisions, and eval-report field name variations. Provide pytest equivalents for every scenario in the four corresponding bats files.

criteria.description: harness/lib/planner.py exists and exposes a callable invoke_planner(mode='new') -> int|str returning the sprint count when given the mock pass scenario.
testSteps: Assert return value or last stdout line == 2

criteria.description: invoke_planner('new') creates non-empty harness-state/product-spec.md and harness-state/sprint-plan.json (valid JSON with .sprints array).
testSteps: Assert product-spec.md exists and has size > 0
json.load sprint-plan.json and assert 'sprints' is a list

criteria.description: invoke_planner logs a mock invocation with agent=planner to MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG.
invoke_planner('extend') uses the extend-mode prompt; the mock log contains the substring 'extend'.

When config.json has projectType=web-frontend and design-spec.md is present, planner emits a success/info message acknowledging the design spec; when absent, it emits a warning. No exception in either case.
testSteps: Run invoke_planner('new') with design-spec.md present and assert no exception
Run again without design-spec.md and assert no exception (warning is acceptable)

criteria.description: harness/lib/contract.py exposes negotiate_contract(sprint_num) and is invoked using a sprint number argument matching bash semantics.
testSteps: Call negotiate_contract(1) with mock pass scenario
Assert no exception and return code 0

criteria.description: negotiate_contract(1) on the pass scenario produces harness-state/sprints/sprint-01/contract.json by copying the proposal upon evaluator acceptance.
negotiate_contract creates both contract-proposal.json and contract-review.json under sprint-01/.
testSteps: Assert both contract-proposal.json and contract-review.json exist

criteria.description: negotiate_contract invokes BOTH the generator and evaluator agents (mock log contains 'agent=generator' and 'agent=evaluator').
testSteps: Assert MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG contains both agent=generator and agent=evaluator entries

criteria.description: With MOCK_CLAUDE_SCENARIO=revise-then-accept, negotiate_contract calls the generator exactly twice (round 1 revise, round 2 accept) and still produces a contract.json.
testSteps: Assert grep count of 'agent=generator' in MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG == 2

criteria.description: negotiate_contract recognises decision values 'accepted', 'accept', 'approved', 'approve' (case-insensitive) read from any of the fields .decision, .reviewVerdict, or .verdict in contract-review.json. Each variant short-circuits the loop on round 1.
criteria.testMethod: unit test with stub review file
testSteps: For each of {accepted, accept, approved, approve, ACCEPTED} placed in each of {decision, reviewVerdict, verdict}, exercise the helper that parses the review file and assert it returns truthy/accepted

criteria.description: The criteria-count helper tolerates three proposal shapes: top-level .criteria array, .features[].acceptanceCriteria (or .features[].criteria), and flat .acceptanceCriteria; returns the correct length for each and 0 when none present.
criteria.testMethod: unit test with stub proposal files
testSteps: Build four stub JSON shapes (criteria, features+acceptanceCriteria, features+criteria, flat acceptanceCriteria)
Call the count helper on each and assert correct integer result

criteria.description: When MAX_CONTRACT_ROUNDS rounds elapse without acceptance, negotiate_contract copies the latest proposal to contract.json and returns 0 (matches bash final fallback behaviour).
testSteps: Use a mock scenario whose evaluator review always returns a non-accepting decision
Assert contract.json exists and equals the last proposal

criteria.description: harness/lib/generator.py exposes invoke_generator(sprint_num, attempt=1) callable from Python.
testSteps: Import and inspect signature has sprint_num and attempt with default 1

criteria.description: invoke_generator on the pass scenario produces sprint-01/status.json containing status='ready-for-eval'.
invoke_generator on the pass scenario produces sprint-01/generator-log.md.
invoke_generator returns 0 on the pass scenario.
invoke_generator returns exactly 2 when status.json reports status='blocked' (matches bash 'return 2').
invoke_generator logs agent=generator to MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG.
On attempt > 1 with eval-report.json present, invoke_generator includes retry/eval-report references in the prompt sent to claude (verified via mock log capturing the prompt argv or stored prompt).
testSteps: Place a fake eval-report.json in sprint-01/
Assert the recorded prompt for that invocation references attempt 2 / eval-report (mirrors bash retry-aware prompt)

criteria.description: When harness-state/design-spec.md is present, invoke_generator's prompt mentions the design spec (matches bash injection).
harness/lib/evaluator.py exposes invoke_evaluator(sprint_num, attempt=1) and invoke_regression() as callables.
invoke_evaluator returns 0 on the pass scenario (eval-report.json overallResult=PASS).
invoke_evaluator returns 1 on the fail-eval scenario.
invoke_evaluator produces harness-state/sprints/sprint-01/eval-report.json containing an overallResult (or tolerated equivalent) field.
testSteps: json.load and assert at least one of overallResult/result/verdict is present

criteria.description: invoke_evaluator logs agent=evaluator to MOCK_CLAUDE_LOG.
Evaluator result parser reads from any of the fields .overallResult, .result, .verdict and accepts case-insensitive 'pass' or 'passed' as success; everything else as failure.
criteria.testMethod: unit test with stub eval-report files
testSteps: For each of {PASS, pass, Passed} placed in each of {overallResult, result, verdict} fields, assert helper returns 0
For 'FAIL', 'unknown', or missing field, assert helper returns 1

criteria.description: Pass/fail/blocking count helpers tolerate the variants used by bash: passCount/pass_count/score.passedCriteria/score.passed; failCount/fail_count/score.failedCriteria/score.failed; blockingFailures/blocking_failures/score.blocking. Missing fields default to 0.
criteria.testMethod: unit test with stub eval-report files
testSteps: Build six stub eval-reports each using a different field-name variant
Call helpers and assert correct integer extraction for each
Assert default of 0 when none of the variants is present

criteria.description: When projectType=web-frontend and a .mcp.json file is present in cwd, invoke_evaluator passes --mcp-config .mcp.json to invoke_claude; otherwise it omits the flag.
testSteps: Set config.json projectType=web-frontend and create empty .mcp.json
Run invoke_evaluator(1, 1) with mock that records argv
Repeat without .mcp.json and assert flag absent

criteria.description: invoke_regression returns 0 on the pass scenario (mock writes harness-state/regression/last-run.json with fail==0).
All four new modules import only Python 3.8+ stdlib (no third-party deps such as requests, click, pydantic).
testSteps: Parse each new module with ast
Assert every import is stdlib (subprocess, pathlib, json, os, sys, shutil, typing, etc.)

criteria.description: No .sh or .ps1 file under harness/ or tests/ is modified by this sprint relative to the merge base; the corresponding bats files remain runnable as before.
Running `python tests/run-all.py layer1` executes the four new test files in addition to the prior ones, and every test passes on the host platform.
testSteps: Assert output mentions test_planner, test_contract, test_generator, test_evaluator modules

criteria.description: Every numbered @test in test-planner.bats, test-contract.bats, test-generator.bats, test-evaluator.bats has a Python equivalent in the corresponding test_*.py file (mapping documented in generator-log.md).
testSteps: List @test names from each bats file
List Python test method names from corresponding test_*.py
Assert one-to-one mapping (Python may add tests but cannot drop bats scenarios)

criteria.description: All Sprint 1, 2, and 3 layer-1 tests still pass (test_utils.py, test_invoke.py, test_git.py) after the new modules and tests are added.
testSteps: Assert no regressions in test_utils, test_invoke, test_git counts/results vs Sprint 3 baseline

outOfScope: harness/orchestrate.py and any top-level CLI wiring (Sprint 5)
Modifying any .sh, .ps1, agent definition, or fixture file
notes: The criteria-count and decision/result/count tolerance helpers (C4-11, C4-12, C4-27, C4-28) are load-bearing: the bash version uses these to absorb real Claude output drift, and the Python port must accept the same field-name and case variants. Implement these as small pure helpers so they can be unit-tested directly without subprocess overhead.
Generator return code 2 for blocked status (C4-18) is part of the orchestrator contract for Sprint 5 and must be preserved exactly.
Use subprocess to launch the mock claude via PATH (already wired in Sprint 2), not by importing mock_claude.py directly, so the integration matches the bash path.
